Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.health

Class ReadinessState

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.health.ReadinessState
public final class ReadinessState extends Object
Shared, observable readiness flag consumed by HealthAggregator to gate the readiness probe.

This is the building block for zero-downtime shutdown. When a microservice begins shutting down it flips the readiness state out of service before the listening connector stops. The HealthAggregator consults this state for the READY probe so /readyz returns 503 (out of service) as soon as shutdown begins — this lets a load balancer / Kubernetes stop routing new traffic to the pod while in-flight requests drain. The LIVE probe (/livez) is intentionally not gated, so Kubernetes does not kill the pod mid-drain.

Resolution:

HealthAggregator and the embedded-server shutdown hooks resolve the state via resolve(BeanStore): a ReadinessState bean registered in the relevant BeanStore wins; otherwise the process-wide shared instance is used. In the standalone microservice runtime the auto-mounted health probe servlet and the server lifecycle component live in different bean stores, so they bridge through the shared instance — the server flips it on stop and the probe observes the flip.

This class is thread-safe; the flag is volatile.

See Also:
  • HealthAggregator
Since:
10.0.0

  • Constructor Details

    • ReadinessState

      public ReadinessState()
      Constructor.

      The new state starts ready.

  • Method Details

    • shared

      public static ReadinessState shared()
      Returns the process-wide shared readiness state.

      Used as the fallback when no ReadinessState bean is registered in the relevant BeanStore. In the standalone microservice runtime this is the bridge between the embedded-server lifecycle component (which flips it on shutdown) and the auto-mounted health probe servlet (which observes the flip), since the two live in separate bean stores.

      Returns:
      The shared instance. Never null.

    • resolve

      public static ReadinessState resolve(BeanStore beanStore)
      Resolves the readiness state from the given bean store, falling back to the shared instance.
      Parameters:
      beanStore - The bean store to consult. Can be null (resolves to the shared instance).
      Returns:
      The resolved readiness state. Never null.

    • isReady

      public boolean isReady()
      Returns whether this service is currently ready to receive traffic.
      Returns:
      true if ready, false if out of service.

    • markReady

      public ReadinessState markReady()
      Marks this service as ready to receive traffic.

      Called when an embedded server starts so a freshly-(re)started service is ready.

      Returns:
      This object.

    • markOutOfService

      public ReadinessState markOutOfService()
      Marks this service as out of service so the readiness probe (/readyz) returns 503.

      Called at the very beginning of shutdown, before the listening connector stops.

      Returns:
      This object.
